guidance_source: provenance and formatting metadata, not topic-tree priors
why: Basic source handling can use non-semantic metadata without deciding excerpt truth from the tree.
risk_if_violated: Semantic tree priors leak into early handling and start biasing the later excerpting stage.

why: This is the stage where excerpt truth must be determined from the source itself and rightful atom grouping.
risk_if_violated: Silent excerpt corruption and loss of taxonomy independence.

why: The same source passage should not split differently merely because the tree is more or less granulated.
Once excerpts are already rightfully frozen, the tree can guide where they should sit.
risk_if_violated: If this stage is confused with excerpting, placement logic will masquerade as excerpt truth.

why: Guidance can be used to detect awkward fit, but not to redefine the excerpt itself silently.
risk_if_violated: The validator becomes a covert second excerpting engine driven by the tree.

guidance_source: tree structure, ranking, ordering, navigation aids
why: This is where structure serves the user by making study navigable after content truth is already protected.
risk_if_violated: If guidance is refused here, the library may become harder to use without improving excerpt integrity.

guidance_source: new placement logic only, not excerpt rewriting
why: Tree refinement should be allowed, but it must preserve underlying excerpt invariance.
risk_if_violated: Tree revision silently rewrites the knowledge base and retroactively changes what was learned.

guidance_source: placement convenience feeding back into excerpt truth
why: The owner's real fear is contamination of the wrong stage by the wrong kind of guidance.
risk_if_violated: The whole A/B/C comparison collapses into a false answer because stage contamination becomes normalized.
